proven.claim: The for-loop over contract.proven executes iff proven.length >= 1.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6 - Boundary and Degenerate Input Analysis
; The for-loop over contract.proven executes iff proven.length >= 1.
; The path condition (contract.proven.length > 0) guarantees at least one iteration.
; Line 368 is inside the loop body — it executes at least once.
;
; To PROVE: proven_length >= 1 cannot be false at line 368.
; Method: assert the path condition, then negate the property. Expect unsat.

(declare-const proven_length Int)

; Path condition: established by the outer if-guard before the loop
(assert (> proven_length 0))

; Transition: for-loop body runs once per element;
; line 368 is inside the body, so it runs proven_length times.
; Computed iteration count = proven_length
(declare-const iteration_count Int)
(assert (= iteration_count proven_length))

; Negate the property: loop executes zero times (line 368 unreachable)
(assert (< iteration_count 1))

(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat
; Proof: proven_length > 0 AND iteration_count = proven_length AND iteration_count < 1
; is unsatisfiable. The path condition guarantees at least one execution.

violations.claim: p.smt2 = "" is a valid JSON string. loadFromDisk assigns it without length validation.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6 - Boundary and Degenerate Input Analysis
; p.smt2 = "" is a valid JSON string. loadFromDisk assigns it without length validation.
;
; Transitions:
;   smt2_char_count = 0
;   p.smt2.split("\n") = [""]    → inner loop prints one blank smt2 line
;   escaped = "".replace(...) = ""
;   console.log(`  Verify: echo '' | z3 -in`)  ← line 368 context
;
; Z3 fed empty stdin via "echo '' | z3 -in" outputs:
;   "(error "line 1 column 0: empty input")"
; The verify command is semantically useless — it will always fail.
; The code prints it without validating that smt2 is non-empty.

(declare-const smt2_char_count Int)     ; character length of p.smt2
(declare-const escaped_char_count Int)  ; character length after replace()
(declare-const verify_cmd_valid Int)    ; 1 = z3 will accept it, 0 = z3 rejects it

; Transition: loadFromDisk assigns smt2 without length validation
(assert (>= smt2_char_count 0))

; Transition: replace() on empty string produces empty string
; escaped_char_count = smt2_char_count when no single quotes present
(assert (= escaped_char_count smt2_char_count))

; Transition: Z3 requires at least one valid SMT-LIB command to produce useful output
; verify_cmd_valid = 1 iff smt2_char_count > 0
(assert (=> (> smt2_char_count 0) (= verify_cmd_valid 1)))
(assert (=> (= smt2_char_count 0) (= verify_cmd_valid 0)))

; Code does NOT validate smt2_char_count before printing the verify command
; Violation: empty smt2 is printed as a verify command that always fails
(assert (= smt2_char_count 0))
(assert (= verify_cmd_valid 0))

(check-sat)
; Expected: sat
; Witness: smt2_char_count=0, escaped_char_count=0, verify_cmd_valid=0
; The printed "Verify: echo '' | z3 -in" is a useless command; Z3 rejects empty input.
; The code silently misleads the user into thinking verification is possible.

violations.claim: contract.proven is loaded entirely from one JSON.parse call.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2 - State Mutation Analysis
; contract.proven is loaded entirely from one JSON.parse call.
; If the disk file is malformed partway through — e.g. proven[0].smt2 = "valid"
; but proven[1].smt2 = null — the first iteration succeeds and the second crashes.
;
; The outer loop does NOT catch exceptions per iteration.
; A null in ANY proven entry causes an unhandled TypeError mid-loop.
; Prior iterations will have already printed partial output (corrupted display).
;
; Encode iterations with smt2 validity per slot.

(declare-const proven_length Int)
(declare-const smt2_valid_count Int)   ; number of entries with non-null smt2
(declare-const smt2_null_count Int)    ; number of entries with null smt2
(declare-const first_crash_index Int)  ; index at which first null smt2 appears

; Path condition
(assert (> proven_length 0))

; Transition: loadFromDisk populates all proven entries from one JSON.parse call
; some entries may have valid smt2, others null — no per-entry validation
(assert (= proven_length (+ smt2_valid_count smt2_null_count)))
(assert (>= smt2_valid_count 0))
(assert (>= smt2_null_count 0))

; Transition: loop processes entries 0..proven_length-1 in order
; first_crash_index is the first index with null smt2
(assert (>= first_crash_index 0))
(assert (< first_crash_index proven_length))
(assert (= first_crash_index smt2_valid_count))  ; all valid entries come first

; Violation: at least one null entry exists, and at least one valid entry precedes it
; (partial output already printed before crash)
(assert (> smt2_null_count 0))
(assert (> smt2_valid_count 0))

(check-sat)
; Expected: sat
; Witness: proven_length=2, smt2_valid_count=1, smt2_null_count=1, first_crash_index=1
; First proven entry prints correctly; second entry's p.smt2.split() throws TypeError.
; Output is left in a partially-rendered, broken state.
